input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

### XAML — `AppThemeBinding`

Replace every hardcoded color with an `AppThemeBinding` that supplies a `Light` and `Dark` value:

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.MainPage"
             BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#1E1E1E}">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="16">

        <Label Text="Welcome"
               FontSize="28"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#000000, Dark=#F0F0F0}" />

        <Label Text="This page adapts to your system theme."
               FontSize="16"
               TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#333333, Dark=#CCCCCC}" />

        <Button Text="Get Started"
                BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#6200EE, Dark=#BB86FC}"
                TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#FFFFFF, Dark=#000000}" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

MAUI reads the device's current `AppTheme` and picks the matching value. When the user toggles system dark mode, every bound property updates automatically — no event handling required.

---

### C# alternative — `SetAppTheme` extension method

The same thing in code-behind or a programmatic page:

```csharp
var page = new ContentPage();
page.SetAppTheme(
    ContentPage.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Colors.White,   // Light
    Color.FromArgb("#1E1E1E")); // Dark

var label = new Label { Text = "Welcome", FontSize = 28 };
label.SetAppTheme(
    Label.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.Black,
    Color.FromArgb("#F0F0F0"));

var button = new Button { Text = "Get Started" };
button.SetAppTheme(
    Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#6200EE"),
    Color.FromArgb("#BB86FC"));
button.SetAppTheme(
    Button.TextColorProperty,
    Colors.White,
    Colors.Black);
```

`SetAppTheme(BindableProperty, lightValue, darkValue)` is the code equivalent of `AppThemeBinding` — it reacts to system theme changes at runtime the same way.

---

### Key points

| Topic | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Trigger** | Responds to `Application.RequestedTheme` (`Light` / `Dark` / `Unspecified`) |
| **Scope** | Works on any `BindableProperty` — colors, images, styles, strings, etc. |
| **Resource dictionaries** | For large apps, define `Color` resources with `AppThemeBinding` in `App.xaml` and reference them with `{StaticResource}` to avoid repetition |
| **Manual override** | Set `Application.Current.UserAppTheme = AppTheme.Dark` to force a theme regardless of the system setting |
